meta.title: About
meta.description: Ponte is a verified network for cross-border trade, operated by 1402 Celsius Ltd. Free to browse, free to post, free to connect.

hero.title: Ponte means
hero.lead: It is a verified network for cross-border trade. Members post offers and requirements for free, the desk checks who is behind them, and the two sides talk to each other directly.

what.label: 01 / What it is
what.body: A public board of vetted deals. Anyone can read it, without an account. Members post an offer or a requirement, the desk reviews it before it goes live, and a counterparty who wants to talk asks for a connection. Nobody pays to be introduced and nobody pays to be found.

i.title: Free where it counts
i.body: Nothing that creates liquidity sits behind a paywall. Browsing, posting and connecting are free. Credits pay for counterparty verification and intelligence work, and every price is printed on the button before you press it.

ii.title: Evidence over opinion
ii.body: Counterparties are checked against company registers and sanctions lists. Sources are named and dated. If a source cannot be reached we record that it was not checked, never that it was clean.

iii.title: Declared, not hidden
iii.body: Every poster declares their role and how far they sit from the principal. Chains are welcome when they are declared. A chain discovered after publication pulls the listing.

desk.label: 03 / The desk
desk.heading: A human reads every listing.
desk.body: Behind Ponte sit three decades in international trade: running trading operations, leading trade intelligence organisations, and sitting between buyers and sellers on four continents. That experience reviews what goes on the board. It is also available on a deal, on a success fee, when a member wants it. That is an option, never a toll.

company.label: 04 / The company
company.body: Ponte is a trading name of 1402 Celsius Ltd, a company incorporated in Bulgaria (Reg. 207314767, VAT BG207314767, 1A Aton Street, Building 6, Plovdiv 4002), part of the same group as 1402 Celsius Ltd, United Kingdom (Reg. 12475013, VAT GB 343 1702 32, 20-22 Wenlock Road, London N1 7GU). Where the desk is engaged on a deal it acts as intermediary only, never as principal, and is remunerated by success fee on closed deals. Payments are processed by Stripe. Correspondence:

cta.heading: Have a deal to place?
cta.body: Post it yourself. It costs nothing, and the desk reads it before anyone else does.

meta.title: Contact
meta.description: Talk to Ponte. Post a deal on the board yourself, or bring one to the desk.

lead.default: Posting a deal does not need us: the board is free and open. Write when you want the desk involved, or when something is not working. We reply within one business day.
lead.retainer: You are considering a Trade Desk Retainer: a standing analyst on call. Tell us the cadence and the kinds of questions you expect, and we will propose a monthly block.

deal.title: Post a deal yourself

detail.lead: This page shows the commercial facts currently supported, what Ponte checked, and what remains unverified. The participant's identity is disclosed only through the controlled introduction.
detail.receiptsIntro: These statements describe specific checks completed for this opportunity. They do not guarantee the transaction or future performance.
detail.unverified1: The counterparty's financial capacity or creditworthiness.
detail.unverified2: The continuing mandate or authority of the individual acting.
detail.unverified3: The ability or willingness to complete on the stated terms.
detail.unverified4: Final price, terms and logistics, which are settled between the parties.

meta.title: Marketplace
meta.description: Submit offers and requirements to the Ponte marketplace. Every published opportunity is reviewed by Ponte, and the public record states which evidence Ponte reviewed and what remains unverified.

hero.heading: Every published opportunity reviewed by Ponte.
hero.intro: Post what you sell or what you need, free. AI and the desk verify every listing before it goes live, you stay anonymous until both sides agree to connect, and connecting costs nothing. Want the deal managed end to end? The desk steps in on a success fee or retainer, only when you ask.

vetted.title: Vetted before live
vetted.body: The public record states which evidence Ponte reviewed and what remains unverified.

anonymous.title: Anonymized always
anonymous.body: Counterparties see the deal, never your name, until both sides agree to connect. Then you each receive the other's contact.

free.title: Free to trade
free.body: Posting, browsing and connecting cost nothing. Bring in the desk only if you want the deal managed end to end, on a success fee or retainer.

how.heading: Five steps. One rule: checked before introduced.

board.empty: The board opens once the first vetted listings clear the desk. Submissions are open now, and early listings get the most attention when it goes live.

mine.heading: Where things stand.

connect.request: A vetted member wants to connect. Accept and you both receive each other's contact. Free.

cta.heading: Prefer to talk it through first?

new.heading: Build it. See it. Publish it.
new.intro: No account needed to build your listing and preview it exactly as it will appear on the board. You sign in only when you publish.

detail.assurance: Reviewed by the Ponte desk before going live. Counterparties stay anonymous until both sides agree to connect; connecting is free.
detail.signInToRead: Sign in free to read the full listing.
detail.machineTranslation: Machine translation. The original wording governs.
detail.footer: Have something to sell or source yourself? <link>Build your listing in three steps</link>, no account needed until you publish.

meta.siteTitle: Ponte. The bridge between buyer and seller.
meta.siteDescription: An invitation-led international trade network. Businesses are checked against company registers and sanctions lists, opportunities are reviewed by Ponte, and introductions are controlled, with identity disclosed only at the introduction stage. The Ponte Desk can manage a mandate on request. Operated by 1402 Celsius Ltd.
meta.ogDescription: Every published opportunity reviewed by Ponte. Businesses checked, introductions controlled. Submit an opportunity, or ask the Ponte Desk to manage a mandate.
meta.twitterDescription: An invitation-led international trade network: businesses checked, opportunities reviewed, introductions controlled.

meta.title: Fees
meta.description: The Ponte marketplace is free: post, get vetted, connect. The desk is optional, on a success fee or a monthly retainer.

pricing.title: The marketplace is free.
pricing.intro: Post, get vetted, connect: no listing fees, no subscriptions, no commission on deals you close yourselves. Credits pay for checking a counterparty. The desk is there if you want it.

marketplace.name: The Marketplace
marketplace.body: Post what you sell or what you need. AI and the desk vet every listing before it goes live. You stay anonymous until both sides agree to connect, then you deal directly. Producers, trading companies and brokers all welcome, roles declared up front.

credits.name: Credits
credits.body: Checking who you are dealing with is the one thing on Ponte that costs money. A check reads the company registers and the OFAC, EU, UN and UK sanctions lists, names every source and dates it, and a person reviews anything that is not clean. Listing checks, write-ups and match alerts stay free.

desk.name: The Desk, per deal
desk.terms: % agreed in writing · paid on closing
desk.body: Want a deal managed end to end? The desk runs verification in depth, NCNDA, negotiation, contracts and closing coordination. The percentage is agreed up front in the fee agreement and is due only when the deal closes. Either side can engage us.

retainer.name: The Desk, on your side
retainer.body: Continuous sourcing or selling support: a standing mandate to find, vet and negotiate on your behalf, month after month. For trading teams, producers entering new markets, and buyers with recurring programmes.

pricing.footnote: Fee levels are agreed privately, in writing, before any work starts. No hidden costs, no charge for introductions you make yourselves on the board.

pwa.installTitle: Add Ponte to your home screen
pwa.installBody: Open the board and post from your phone, with no browser in the way.
pwa.iosBody: Tap the share button in Safari, then choose Add to Home Screen.
pwa.offlineBody: Ponte needs a connection to show live listings and to run a verification. Nothing you were doing has been lost. Reconnect and carry on.
pwa.offlineHint: This page was saved on your device

meta.title: Verify a trade counterparty
meta.description: How Ponte verifies a company: the public registry record, EU VAT through VIES, the LEI through GLEIF, and screening against the OFAC, EU, UN and UK OFSI sanctions lists. What each level checks, and what a verification does not mean.

hero.title: Verify a trade counterparty.
hero.intro: Before cargo or money moves, you want to know that the company on the contract exists, is still on the register, and does not appear on a sanctions list. Ponte checks that against named public sources and records what was found, on the date it was found. This page sets out exactly what is checked at each level, and what the result does not tell you.

levels.heading: The four levels
levels.lead: Each level adds evidence to the one below it. A member holds the highest level they have completed, and that level is shown wherever they appear.

l1.name: Identity
l1.body: The person is who they say they are.
l1.c2: An identity document reviewed by the desk.

l2.name: Business
l2.body: The company exists on a public register, and neither the company nor its officers appear on a sanctions list.
l2.c1: The company registry record: UK Companies House for a company registered in the United Kingdom, OpenCorporates for other jurisdictions.
l2.c2: EU VAT through VIES, where a VAT number is supplied.
l2.c3: The Legal Entity Identifier through GLEIF, where an LEI is supplied.
l2.c4: Sanctions screening of the company and of every officer named on the register.

l3.name: Activity
l3.body: The company has traded, on the evidence it supplies.
l3.c1: Trade documents the member provides themselves.
l3.c2: Documents read and cross checked against the registry record and the declared business.
l3.c3: Reviewed by a person before the level is granted.

l4.name: Institutional
l4.c1: Scope agreed in writing before any work starts.
l4.c2: For counterparties, lenders and insurers who need more than the levels above.

sanctions.heading: Sanctions screening
sanctions.lead: The company name and every officer name are screened against four published lists:
sanctions.l1: OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List and the OFAC Consolidated Sanctions List, United States Department of the Treasury.
sanctions.l3: United Nations Security Council Consolidated List.
sanctions.l4: UK OFSI Consolidated List of financial sanctions targets.
sanctions.matching: Names are compared with an allowance for spelling and transliteration, so a candidate is a reason to look more closely, not a finding on its own. Every candidate is read by a person before anything is decided.
sanctions.refresh: Screening is not a one time event. When these lists change, members already verified are screened again against the updated lists, and a new candidate reopens the case.

process.heading: How a case is decided
process.auto: Where every source answers, and the answers agree with the details the member declared, the result is recorded without a person needing to intervene.
process.review: Where anything is unclear, incomplete or contradictory, the case goes to the desk. The member is emailed with the outcome.
process.human: An approval and a rejection are always decided by a person. Nothing is ever rejected automatically.

sources.heading: What the result is built on
sources.body: Company registers, the EU VIES VAT service, the GLEIF LEI record, the published sanctions lists named above, and documents a member supplies about their own business. Every check records the source it came from and the date it was run, and a source that could not be reached is reported as not checked rather than treated as clean.

disclaimer.heading: What a verification does not mean

cta.heading: Run a business check
cta.body: A Level 2 check runs on the company name and its country of registration, with a registration number or VAT number where you have one.

meta.title: Run a business verification
meta.description: Check a company against its public registry record, EU VAT, the GLEIF LEI record and the published sanctions lists.

request.heading: Business verification
request.intro: This check reads the company registry record, checks EU VAT through VIES and the LEI through GLEIF where you supply them, and screens the company and its officers against the OFAC, EU, UN and UK OFSI sanctions lists.

signedOut.heading: Sign in to run a check
signedOut.body: A verification runs against your account, so the result and the credits it spends stay on your record.

balance.label: Your balance
balance.short: You do not have enough credits for this check.

fields.namePlaceholder: The registered name, as written on the register
fields.regHint: A registration number is the strongest match available. Give it where you have it.
fields.vatHint: An EU VAT number is checked through VIES. Leave this blank if there is none.

errors.name: Enter the company name.
errors.credits: You do not have enough credits for this check.
errors.signIn: Please sign in and try again.
errors.rateLimit: That is a lot of checks in a short time. Please try again later.
errors.generic: The check could not be completed. Please try again.

select.title: More than one company matches that name
select.body: The register holds several companies under the name you entered, so the subject is not identified yet. Choose the one you mean and the check will continue on that company: registry record, VAT, LEI, and sanctions screening of the company and its officers.
select.noCharge: This check is already paid for. Choosing a company finishes it and spends no further credits.
select.capped: The register reports {total} matches and the first {shown} are listed here. If the company you want is not among them, run the check again with its registration number.
select.noNumber: No registration number was published for this match, so it cannot be checked. Run the check again with the number.

errors.notFound: That check could not be found on your account.
errors.notSelectable: That check is no longer waiting for a company to be chosen.
errors.unknownCandidate: That company was not one of the matches offered. Choose one from the list.
errors.generic: The check could not be continued. Please try again.

result.verifiedBody: The registry record is active, the details you gave match it, and no sanctions candidates were found. The result is on your record.
result.reviewBody: Something in this check needs a person to look at it. A member of the desk will read the case, compare the sources, and decide it. You will be emailed with the outcome. Nothing is decided automatically.
result.failedBody: The check stopped before a result could be recorded. Where the fault was on our side, the credits are returned to your balance.

request.disclaimerHeading: What this result does not mean
